flashcards.front: What is the primary advantage of skip lists over balanced search trees?
flashcards.back: Simpler implementation while maintaining average O(log n) time complexity for key operations.

flashcards.front: Who developed skip lists and when?
What is the worst-case time complexity for search in skip lists?
flashcards.back: O(n) when all elements end up in the same level.

flashcards.front: How do skip lists achieve probabilistic balancing?
flashcards.back: Through random level assignment for new nodes using a coin flip algorithm.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the '-inf' header node in skip lists?
flashcards.back: Acts as starting point for all search/insert operations across all levels.

flashcards.front: What data structure do skip lists extend conceptually?
flashcards.back: Sorted linked lists with added express lanes through multiple layers.

flashcards.front: What is the expected number of levels in a skip list with n elements?
flashcards.back: O(log n) levels with high probability.

flashcards.front: How does insertion work in skip lists?
flashcards.back: 1. Find position 2. Randomly determine level 3. Insert and update pointers at each level.

flashcards.front: Why are skip lists considered memory-efficient?
flashcards.back: Most nodes only have 1-2 pointers, unlike balanced trees that store multiple balance factors.

flashcards.front: What real-world systems use skip lists?
flashcards.back: Redis database (sorted sets), Lucene search engine, and distributed systems.

flashcards.front: How is deletion handled in skip lists?
flashcards.back: Similar to insertion but removing nodes from all levels they appear in.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of skip lists?
flashcards.back: O(n) on average due to probabilistic level distribution.
